headline: The life and death of the Ahmed Abbas 714 building in Beirut
description: The residential building was destroyed on March 18 by an Israeli airstrike. Israel claimed, without providing evidence, that Hezbollah money was being hidden in its basement. With the destruction of these 36 apartments, a piece of Lebanese life has vanished.

Israel says it killed head of Hamas' military wing
description: The Israeli military and Hamas officials confirmed the death of Ezz al-Din al-Haddad on Saturday after an Israeli strike targeted a residential building in Gaza City.

headline: A smooth Trump-Xi summit masked deeper competing motives
description: By inviting Donald Trump, mired in his conflict with Iran, the Chinese president positioned himself as a symbol of stability in contrast to the turmoil fueled by Washington.

headline: Pope Leo to visit France in September
description: The Vatican announced that the pope's state visit will take place from September 25 to 28. He is expected to visit Paris and Lourdes, in southwestern France.

headline: How Corsica's schools became the epicenter of a chess revolution
description: On the Île de Beauté in Corsica, chess players began learning the game since they were children, with the island counting 16 times more registered players per capita than the rest of France.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
description: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

headline: Trump, with ambitions for Mars, Canada and Greenland, tries to reclaim frontier myth of pioneer America
description: Since the very beginning, the US has based its growth on the conquest of new geographical or political frontiers. In this spirit, the current president speaks of other lands – on Earth and in space – while the rise of artificial intelligence opens up uncharted territories.

headline: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
description: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.
